It belongs to the genus Disynaphia, which is a group of flowering plants. Disynaphia montevidensis is native to South America, specifically in the region of Uruguay and parts of Argentina. The plant is characterized by its unique bell-shaped flowers and is often found growing in sandy or rocky areas. Within the Campanulaceae family, Disynaphia montevidensis is one of the lesser-known species.
